courses.title: Introduction to Computer Programming for Scientists and Engineers (MATLAB)

notes: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on L&S General Education requirements: For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps: Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements” Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley , and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). Click on “View Agreements”. Under “View Agreement by” select “ General Education/Breadth ”. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement” Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. Review all available majors at UC Berkeley .

Analytics is a high demand major. Required lower-division courses: Math 53, Math 54, Econ 1, 2 or 3C, and UGBA 10X must be completed prior to acceptance to the major and all must be taken for a letter grade. Highly Recommend: Engin 7, Comp Sci 61A, Data C8, and Data C88

AP Math: Calculus AB with a score of 3 or higher (or AP Math: Calculus BC score of 3) will be accepted by the department for the Math 51 requirement at Berkeley. AP Math: Calculus BC score of 4 or 5 will be accepted by the major for the Math 52 requirement at Berkeley.

In the Analytics major, students will develop solid quantitative and data-driven problem solving skills through core courses in computing, probability/statistics, optimization, and data analytics, and will learn how to apply these skills in solving problems in an area of their choice. We outline four possible concentrations below, but many other areas can also benefit from deploying analytical methods. Students may design their own concentrations according to their interests, with guidance and approval from the Analytics faculty advisor in the Industrial Engineering and Operations Research (IEOR) Department. Admission to Berkeley with the stated intent to major in Analytics does not guarantee acceptance into the major, a departmental application is required. Transfer students must apply to the Analytics major by the end of their first semester at UC Berkeley. Application information can be found at https://ieor.berkeley.edu/academics/ba-orms/ We do not accept applications from graduating seniors or students in their fourth (or later) year of studies, even if it is not the student's first major. Prerequisite Coursework: Prior to transferring to UC Berkeley, students are required to complete the following courses: course equivalents of Math 51 & Math 52 (prerequisites to Math 53 and Math 54) The department strongly recommends that the following courses are also completed prior to transferring to UC Berkeley. Any courses that are not completed prior to transfer must be completed by the end of the student's first term at UC Berkeley. Math 53, Math 54, Econ 1, 2 or 3C, and UGBA 10X All prerequisite courses must be taken for a letter grade. Transfer students must complete their prerequisite courses and apply to the major at the end of the first semester in which they are enrolled at UC Berkeley. UC Summer session prior to the academic year does not count as the first semester. Engin 7, Comp Sci 61A, and Data C8 may be taken after acceptance to the major. Data C88 must be taken at Berkeley. For more information: Student Affairs Officer Department of Operations Research and Industrial Engineering (510) 642-5485 ieor-student-services@berkeley.edu ORMS - UC Berkeley IEOR Department - Industrial Engineering & Operations Research
